heading: Authority of Inspectors General
label: 11298 Authority of Inspectors General
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 5780a of Title 42Section was enacted as part of the Crime Control Act of 1990, and not as part of the Missing Children’s Assistance Act which comprises this subchapter, nor as part of the Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention Act of 1974 which comprises this chapter.

L. 117–286, § 4(b)(59)(A)Subsec. (b)(1). , substituted “chapter 4 of title 5.” for “the Inspector General Act of 1978 (5 U.S.C. App.).”Pub. L. 117–286, § 4(b)(59)(B)

children.text: by conducting reviews of inactive case files to develop recommendations for further investigations; and

children.heading: Priority
children.label: 1 Priority
children.text: An Inspector General may not permit staff to engage in activities described in subsection (a) if such activities will interfere with the duties of the Inspector General under chapter 4 of title 5.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 2 Funding
children.text: No additional funds are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section.
